$schema_note: Documentation-only evidence artifact for task F0-011. Records the CURRENT (as of baseline commit) gap between required backup/restore coverage and evidenced coverage, per data category. Does not authorize any backup configuration change or restore execution. All 'proposed_target' values are PoC/business-approval proposals, not established facts, per the design document's §9.4/§16.2.
task: F0-011 — Object Storage and Backup Migration Design
phase: F0 — Baseline, Program Control, and Architecture Validation

generated_from.coverage_statement: BOUNDED. Covers the data categories named in the F0-011 task's backup-architecture requirement (A-H) to the extent evidenced by repository and production-topology-document inspection. Categories without any evidenced implementation are recorded as gaps, not assumed absent beyond what the cited sources state.

evidence_classification_legend.VERIFIED_PRODUCTION_OBSERVED: Directly observed in a documented production evidence-gathering pass (F0-002/F0-006)
evidence_classification_legend.VERIFIED_REPOSITORY: Confirmed present/absent in repository source code
evidence_classification_legend.UNVERIFIED_PRODUCTION: Neither confirmed present nor confirmed absent in production; repository gives no conclusive signal
evidence_classification_legend.INFERRED: A reasoned conclusion from indirect evidence, explicitly not a direct observation
evidence_classification_legend.NOT_FOUND: Actively searched for and not found — a stronger claim than UNVERIFIED, used when a targeted search specifically for this item returned nothing

categories.current_coverage: Cron-driven backup to /root/noramedi-backups via /usr/local/sbin/noramedi-db-backup.sh (external to repository), 7-day declared retention
categories.gap: Backup directory is on the SAME HOST as the database — no offsite copy found or supplied
categories.proposed_target_rpo: <=24h now; <=15min once PITR implemented (proposal, not authoritative)
categories.restore_test_status: Capability exists (runRestoreTest(), backupService.ts:167-277); no durable evidence of actual execution
categories.backup_encryption_status: UNVERIFIED_PRODUCTION — backup script itself not in repository, could not be inspected

categories.gap: Recovery resolution is bounded only by backup frequency (~11h observed interval per RISK_REGISTER R-031), not continuous
categories.proposed_target_rto: Not proposed separately from GAP-A's database RTO
categories.restore_test_status: N/A — cannot test PITR that does not exist

categories.category: Object storage / file-tree backup (attachments, imaging, lab attachments)
categories.current_coverage: None found — no file-tree backup script, job, or process exists anywhere in the repository
categories.evidence_class: NOT_FOUND (repository-wide search for backup-related patterns outside backupService.ts's own trio returned nothing)
categories.gap: Complete — this is the single largest gap identified by this task. Patient attachments, lab attachments, and imaging images (the highest-sensitivity storage flows in f0-011-storage-flow-inventory.json) have zero backup coverage of file bytes today
categories.linked_risk: R-029 (storage architecture gap); no dedicated risk-register row exists yet specifically for 'no file backup' as distinct from 'no object storage' — this document recommends RISK_REGISTER.md track this as its own line item, not implemented here

categories.category: Local filesystem during migration (uploads/ tree, pre-migration source of truth)
categories.current_coverage: None — same as GAP-C; the migration design (design doc §10) itself requires a one-time full inventory/checksum snapshot before any backfill begins, which does not exist today
categories.gap: No inventory or checksum baseline exists for the current uploads/ tree, which the migration design names as a stage-1 precondition
categories.proposed_target_rpo: N/A — this is a one-time snapshot requirement, not an ongoing backup cadence

categories.current_coverage: Environment-variable-based, per existing fileStorage.ts pattern (S3_BUCKET, S3_REGION, S3_ENDPOINT, S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID, S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, S3_FORCE_PATH_STYLE); these vars are confirmed absent from both production (ENVIRONMENT_MATRIX.md) and server/.env.example
categories.gap: S3 configuration vars are undocumented in .env.example — an operator following only the example would not discover them; this is a documentation gap, not a backup gap per se

categories.current_coverage: Implicitly covered by the existing PostgreSQL backup (GAP-A) since AuditLog is a standard Postgres table — no separate mechanism exists or is proposed
categories.evidence_class: INFERRED (follows directly from AuditLog being a Postgres table, not independently verified as included in the DB backup scope)
categories.gap: None beyond GAP-A's own gaps (offsite copy, PITR, restore-test evidence) — no AuditLog-specific gap identified
categories.migration_priority: N/A — no independent action needed beyond closing GAP-A

categories.current_coverage: None — same as GAP-C, at higher sensitivity (VERY_HIGH classification, f0-011-storage-flow-inventory.json flow F011-005/F011-006) and plausibly larger file sizes
categories.gap: Complete — no backup of imaging binaries exists; ImagingStudy is documented as immutable/never-hard-deleted in application logic, which makes the absence of any backup particularly significant since the application's own design assumes this data persists indefinitely
categories.proposed_target_rto: <=4h (proposal); imaging-specific transfer duration is an additional PoC measurement (design doc §16.2)

categories.category: Export artifacts where legally required (clinic bulk export, patient privacy export)
categories.current_coverage: Not backed up, but time-bound by design (15-minute and 1-hour TTL cleanup jobs, f0-011-storage-flow-inventory.json flows F011-008/F011-010/F011-012)
categories.gap: No gap identified as a *backup* requirement — these are derived, short-lived artifacts regenerable from source data, not a source of truth; this document explicitly does not recommend backing them up (design doc §9.3)
categories.proposed_target_rpo: N/A — not recommended as a backup target
categories.migration_priority: N/A — deliberately excluded from backup scope by design, not an oversight
